title: Ethana Capability Validation Output
description: Machine-readable output produced by the ethana-capability-validation skill. This schema governs the inter-skill payload passed from Capability Validation to Solution Mapping, Feature Mapping, and all downstream agents that consume validated capability status.

capability_name.description: Exact name of the capability as it appears in canonical-product-model.md

validated_status.description: Status adjudicated from canonical-product-model.md. Unresolved indicates canonical model has no entry and escalation is required.

canonical_entry_verbatim.description: Verbatim quote of the canonical-product-model.md entry for this capability. Required when validated_status is Production, In Build, or Aspirational.

mandatory_caveats.description: Mandatory caveats from the canonical model entry that must accompany all customer-facing claims about this capability.

scope_boundaries.description: Confirmed scope (covers) and exclusions (does not cover) as derived from canonical-product-model.md.

ecs.description: Evidence Confidence Score. 0 when validated_status is In Build or Aspirational and a Production claim is being evaluated (Path B/C cap applies).

ecs_band.description: ECS band derived from the score. Insufficient = ECS 0-19; Contested = 20-44; Canonical-only = 45-69; Corroborated = 70-84; Authoritative = 85-100.

ecs_path.description: ECS computation path selected based on claimed status vs. canonical status. A = canonical confirms claimed status. B = canonical shows In Build, Production claimed. C = canonical shows Aspirational, any claim. D = canonical entry absent. E = sub-capability split required. F = contested (multiple sources with significant conflicts).

ecs_arithmetic.description: Human-readable ECS arithmetic showing base score plus all adjustments applied. Required for Dimension 3 scoring.

allowed_claims.description: Claims approved for use in downstream outputs. Each entry has a specific CPL. Aspirational capabilities must produce an empty array.

claim_text.description: Exact, quotable claim text ready for use in a downstream output without further rewriting.

cpl.description: CPL-1 = Unrestricted (caveat embedded). CPL-2 = Advisory-restricted (caveat required before formal use). CPL-3 = Conversation-only (Discovery and Internal Briefing only). CPL-4 = Internal-only.

permitted_contexts.description: Contexts in which this claim may be used without modification.

required_caveat.description: Caveat text that must accompany this claim before formal use. Empty string if caveat is already embedded in claim_text (CPL-1).

evidence_basis.description: Source and ECS basis for this claim (e.g., 'canonical-product-model.md — Production entry; ECS 85').

prohibited_claims.description: Claims that must not appear in any downstream output at any CPL level. All entries are CPL-5.

claim_text.description: The specific claim or claim pattern that is prohibited.

cpl.description: All prohibited claims are CPL-5. A CPL-5 claim in Section 4 (Allowed Claims) triggers Hard Disqualifier HQ7.

prohibition_reason.description: Reason from the approved prohibition list: 'In Build, not yet available', 'Aspirational capability', 'Scope expansion', 'Certification not held', 'Performance not confirmed', 'Integration not confirmed'.

risk_if_used.description: Specific business or regulatory risk if this claim is used in a customer-facing context.

source_of_claim.description: Where this claim was observed (e.g., 'Marketing playbook, page 7', 'Advisory team's proposed competitive response').

contradictions_count.description: Number of source contradictions documented in Section 6 (Contradiction Log). Must match the count of 'Contradicts' or 'Expands' rows in sources_checked.

sources_checked.description: All sources consulted during validation. canonical-product-model.md must appear first and must always be present.

source_name.description: Exact file name or document reference.

authority_level.description: Primary = canonical-product-model.md only. Secondary = Approved Secondary sources (product-architecture-investigation.md, use-cases.md). Reference Only = all other sources including marketing playbooks, roadmap slides.

claim_made.description: The specific claim or entry from this source relevant to the capability being validated. Use 'Silent' if the source has no relevant entry.

consistent_with_canonical.description: Yes = consistent. Silent = no relevant entry. Contradicts = directly conflicts with canonical status or properties. Expands = extends scope beyond what canonical model confirms.

escalation_required.description: True when ECS < 45 or validated_status is Unresolved or a contradiction requires product team resolution.

recipient.description: Named escalation recipient (e.g., 'canonical model maintainer', 'Ethana engineering lead', 'Marketing team').

specific_question.description: The exact question requiring resolution — specific enough for the recipient to answer without additional research.

interim_position.description: What may and may not be said while escalation is pending.

downstream_blocks.description: Downstream output types blocked pending escalation resolution.

hard_disqualifiers_triggered.description: List of hard disqualifiers triggered. Empty array = no disqualifiers. Any entry blocks output release.

phase_9_gate_completed.description: Confirms all 7 Phase 9 gate steps were completed. False or absent triggers Hard Disqualifier HQ6.

validation_date.description: ISO 8601 date on which this validation was performed.

claim_context.description: The commercial context in which the validated claims will be used.

allowed_claims.description: Aspirational capabilities produce zero allowed claims. Section 4 must be empty.
